channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Treasurer remains non-committal on potential tax offset for Australian workers
item.description: Ahead of next week’s Federal Budget, reports suggest the Australian Government may introduce an 'earned income offset' of between $200 and $300 for wage and salary earners to help manage the cost-of-l...

item.title: Non-payment of FASTag at barrier-less toll plazas may suspend vehicle services until dues are cleared
item.description: The government has clarified that failure to pay tolls through FASTag at barrier-less toll plazas on the National Highway network will lead to suspension of all FASTag-linked services for the concerne...

item.title: KCB Bank boosts Tanzania–Kenya trade ties amid regional integration efforts
item.description: KCB Bank has pledged to play a stronger role in connecting Tanzania and Kenya through trade and investment, acting as a key financial bridge for regional economic integration. The bank’s CEO, Paul Rus...

item.title: Concerns Over Long-Term Zoning Monopoly in Gombe State Politics
item.description: Since Nigeria return to democracy for 1999, Gombe State don dey show say political power dey concentrated for Gombe North, particularly Gombe Local Government. For 24 years now, governors from this on...

item.title: University cleaner awarded £264,000 after unfair dismissal dispute
item.description: A 72-year-old part-time cleaner, Peak Ong, has been awarded over £260,000 in compensation after an employment tribunal ruled she was unfairly dismissed from Aberystwyth University. Ong, who worked at ...

item.title: Atin Ito group rejects China’s Sandy Cay claims, calls landing staged and allegations misleading
item.description: The Atin Ito coalition criticised China’s reported landing on Pag-asa Cay 2 (Sandy Cay) in the West Philippine Sea, describing it as a “copycat” action following a prior civilian-led Filipino mission....

item.title: PTI Punjab Committee Members Confirmed Amid Internal Debate
item.description: Islamabad – PTI Secretary General Salman Akram Raja has confirmed his decision regarding the composition of the newly formed Punjab Coordination and Monitoring Committee, stating clearly that no curre...

item.title: BJP Criticises Mamata Banerjee for Not Resigning Despite West Bengal Poll Defeat
item.description: In the aftermath of the West Bengal elections, the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has strongly criticised Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee for refusing to resign despite her party, Trinamool Congress (TMC...

item.title: DMK leads in postal ballot counting across nine constituencies in Salem and Namakkal
item.description: The Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) has secured the highest number of postal ballot votes in nine Assembly constituencies across Salem and Namakkal districts, indicating an early advantage in these re...

item.title: Six Barrie Residents Face Charges Over $300,000 Retail Theft at 400 Market
item.description: Authorities in Barrie, Ontario, have charged six individuals in connection with a major theft at a local retail store, 400 Market. The suspects are accused of stealing merchandise valued at approximat...

item.title: LTFRB orders 9 motorcycle taxi companies to explain excess and missing ride-hailing units
item.description: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) has issued show cause orders (SCOs) to nine motorcycle taxi platform providers (MTPPs) following discrepancies in the number of authori...

item.title: Khalwale and Maina Exchange Humorous Remarks During Senate Debate on Blue Economy
item.description: During a lively session in the Senate on Tuesday, May 5, Kakamega Senator Boni Khalwale claimed that nominated Senator Veronica Maina has shown a crush on him several times. The incident followed a di...

item.title: Western Cape schools in Eden and Central Karoo close for two days due to heavy rain warning
item.description: Schools in parts of the Western Cape’s Eden and Central Karoo districts will be closed for two days as authorities prepare for severe weather conditions expected this week. The provincial Department o...

item.title: Mbappe Takes Club-Approved Break Amid Clasico Build-Up, Fans React
item.description: Real Madrid striker Kylian Mbappe dey face plenty wahala from fans after him waka go Sardinia for holiday, just days before the big Clasico against Barcelona. Even though him dey recover from hamstrin...

item.title: Saskatchewan Introduces Public Surveys to Explore Youth Social Media Restrictions
item.description: The Saskatchewan government has launched a public engagement initiative focused on evaluating potential restrictions on youth access to social media. As part of this process, the province has released...

item.title: The Geopolitics of Oil: Energy as a Strategic Tool in Global Conflicts
item.description: The ongoing conflict with Iran illustrates that modern warfare extends far beyond missiles and bombs. Energy, particularly oil, has become a central instrument of geopolitical power. The United States...

item.title: Nigeria National Assembly to Investigate Xenophobic Attacks on Nigerians in South Africa
item.description: The Nigerian National Assembly has resolved to establish a joint ad hoc committee comprising members from both the Senate and the House of Representatives to investigate the increasing incidents of xe...

item.title: Lara, Emil, and Ahmad Showcase Loyalty and Conflict in New ABS-CBN Series 'Blood vs Duty'
item.description: ABS-CBN continues to showcase Filipino talent through its latest television series, “Blood vs Duty,” featuring prominent actors Lara Angeles, Emil Azuelo, and Ahmad Mustapha. The show explores themes ...

item.title: Australia's Big Four Banks Raise Home Loan Rates Following RBA Hike
item.description: Following the Reserve Bank of Australia’s recent decision to lift the cash rate from 4.1% to 4.35% in an 8-1 vote, all of the Big Four banks have announced corresponding increases to their home loan v...

item.title: Shopify Reports US$581 Million Q1 Loss While Expanding AI Initiatives
item.description: Shopify has reported a first-quarter loss of US$581 million, highlighting the challenges it faces even as it expands its artificial intelligence (AI) initiatives. The e-commerce platform, widely used ...
